commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers require storage/privacy/product decisions and a conflicting-branch refresh, not a narrow automated repair.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a concrete cross-agent memory boundary concern when session-memory sources share the same SQLite store.; Review finding: [P1] Keep session-memory chunks out of shared stores — src/agents/memory-search.ts:402-407... (sha=d7e7287c1f6dc74537b834fc716aa72506cf5945)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes memory-search resolution to use a shared SQLite store for agents with the same workspace, adds a shared-store registry helper, exports related SDK helpers, and adds config tests for shared store hashing.

PR surface: Source +140, Tests +74. Total +214 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: with sessionMemory enabled and sources including both memory and sessions, the PR routes same-workspace agents to one DB while current session sync indexes files by agentId and current search/schema do not filter by agent membership.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers require storage/privacy/product decisions and a conflicting-branch refresh, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: feat(memory): shared memory store by directory binding
target.body:
## Summary

Agents pointing to the same workspace directory share one physical SQLite store. The store path is derived from a deterministic sha256 hash of the workspace directory + extraPaths — no chunk-level ACL needed.

## Changes

### Core logic (`memory-search.ts`)
- `resolveMemorySearchConfig()` overrides `store.databasePath` to a shared path: `state/memory/shared-{hash}.sqlite`
- `computeSharedScopeHash()` computes a deterministic 16-char hash from workspaceDir + extraPaths, with deduplication and path normalization
- `mergeConfig()` returns the new `sharedStorePath` field
- `ResolvedMemorySearchConfig` type updated

### SDK exports
- `engine-foundation.ts` — export `computeSharedScopeHash`, `resolveMemorySearchConfig`, `ResolvedMemorySearchConfig`
- `engine-storage.ts` — export `resolveStateDir`, `resolveAgentWorkspaceDir`

### Tests (`memory-search.test.ts`)
- Same workspace → same store path (shared-{16hex}.sqlite)
- Different workspaces → different store paths
- Relative/absolute extraPath normalization produces consistent hashes

## Design

No `agent_ids` column, no chunk-level filtering. Directory binding is the access boundary — agents sharing a workspace see the same memory index. Schema, search, write, and clear all work unmodified on the shared file.

Closes #95724
target.author: xmoxmo
